examples.english:
baynahumā barzaḵun lā yabġiyāni
Between them is a barrier [so] neither of them transgresses.

senses.gloss: netherworld; barzakh; hades; purgatory (otherworldly existence, generally temporal, which may include elements of punishment and/or reward)

etymology.text: From Parthian *bwrz-ʾxw (/⁠burz-axw⁠/, “height, unsurmountable passage”), from Parthian bwrz (/⁠burz⁠/, “high”) + ʾxw (/⁠axw⁠/, “world”).

body.text: A thing that intervenes between any two things:
a thing that makes a separation, between two things:
(S, A, L, K:) so in the Kur lv. 20: pl.
interval between the present life and that which is to come,
from the period of death to the resurrection,
upon which he who dies enters;
state, from the day of death to the day of resurrection:
What is between the beginning of faith,
(L, K,) which is the acknowledgment, or confession, of God, (L,)
(L, K,) which is the removal of what is hurtful from the road: (L:) or
what is between doubt and certainty.
